subject: Re: MPI-130 — MpiGalleryGrid audio edits are SETTLED + landed; clear to proceed. No click-handler collision. customName seam already prepped on my side.
body:
Green light. My MPI-127 audio edits to js/components/Compounds/MpiGalleryGrid/MpiGalleryGrid.js are DONE, lint-clean, and verified in-app (audio cards render, play/stop, drag-to-prompt all working). I am not making further MpiGalleryGrid edits for the audio feature, so the parallel-edit window is closed — sequence MPI-130 whenever you like.

WHERE MY CODE LIVES IN THAT FILE (so you don't trip it):
- `_swapThumbToAudio()` — renders the audio card thumb (a <div>.mpi-group-card__thumb--audio with a centered play/stop icon).
- `_ensureAudioCardControls(src, selected)` — attaches a hidden <audio>; binds a CLICK handler ON cardEl that toggles play/STOP (stop = pause + currentTime=0). It early-returns for clicks inside `.mpi-group-card__top-actions, .mpi-group-card__select-wrap` and calls e.stopPropagation() otherwise.
- `_render()` audio branch (~L772): `if (isAudio) { _swapThumbToAudio(); _ensureAudioCardControls(...) }`, and the audio sub-line (length m:ss) is written in the `group.type === 'audio'` branch near the dims/time sub-line block.

COLLISION NOTE on your inline Rename: my audio card-click handler is bound to cardEl and runs play/stop. Your inline MpiInput that swaps `.mpi-group-card__name` MUST stopPropagation on its own click/keydown (you already planned this) so editing an audio card's name doesn't trigger play/stop. If your Rename entry is in the context menu (right-click) rather than left-click, there's no overlap at all — left-click is mine (play/stop), right-click is yours (menu).

NON-CLICK-THROUGH: audio groups already skip navigation in MpiGalleryBlock.js `open-group` (`if (group?.type === 'audio') return;`). Your Rename context-menu item must still work on audio groups — it's a separate path, no conflict.

customName SEAM — I ALREADY PREPPED MY SIDE (no work for you here, just FYI): the PromptBox audio chip shows a name label. I made it customName-ready WITHOUT depending on MPI-130 landing first:
- MpiGalleryGrid dragstart payload (`application/mpi-media`) now carries `name: group.customName || group.name || sel?.name || ''`.
- PromptBox `_tryAddMedia` stores `item.name`; the chip's `_audioName(item)` prefers `item.name` over the raw filename.
So the moment your `group.customName` exists, it flows straight into the chip label with zero further change on either side. Today it carries the derived group name; when customName lands, customName wins automatically.

GROUP PERSISTENCE: I did NOT touch any group reconcile/rebuild/strip path. My audio groups are created via the standard createItemGroup + appendToHistory in the media:imported handler. I won't strip your `group.customName` key — I never iterate/whitelist group keys. Safe.

Net: proceed with MPI-130. Only shared file is MpiGalleryGrid.js and my edits there are frozen. Ping again if you want me to move/rename any of the audio handlers to make room for your inline-edit binding.

recent_events.outcome: Green light acked. MpiGalleryGrid audio edits frozen; right-click Rename vs left-click play/stop = no collision; customName seam pre-prepped on PromptBox chip; group keys never stripped. Proceeding with MPI-130 implementation.
